home.navDescription: This app demonstrates real SwiftUI TabView with per-tab NavigationStack. Each page has its own WebView instance.
home.navHint: Try switching tabs — state is preserved. Navigate from Features to a detail page and back.
list.item1.title: Alpine.js Rendering
list.item2.title: Dual Thread Model
list.item3.title: Tab Navigation
list.item4.title: Stack Navigation
list.item5.title: File System
detail.desc1: The View Layer uses Alpine.js CSP build for reactive DOM updates. No eval() or Function() needed — safe for WebView CSP policies. Templates use x-data, x-text, x-show, x-for directives.
detail.desc2: Logic runs in JSContext (no DOM access). Views run in WebView (no host API access). Communication flows through setData() patches and event messages. This is the same architecture as WeChat Mini Programs.
detail.desc3: The manifest defines a tab_bar with 2–5 tabs. The host renders a real SwiftUI TabView. Each tab maintains its own navigation state independently. Tab state is preserved when switching.
detail.desc4: Each tab has a real SwiftUI NavigationStack. Pages are pushed with navigateTo() and popped with navigateBack(). Each page gets its own WebView instance — fully isolated. Max depth: 10 pages per stack.
detail.desc5: OPFS-style sandboxed file system with getDirectoryHandle(), getFileHandle(), read(), write(), entries(), and removeEntry(). Per-app isolation with path traversal prevention.
profile.navInfo: Profile tab is active. State is preserved when switching tabs.
